knowledge_atoms.text: Cool! I have lots of comic books about space. When are you going?
How can you learn to swim without going to a pool?
I'm going to buy a new comic book.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 我打算买一本新的漫画书。

knowledge_atoms.text: I'm going to draw some pictures.
I'm going to draw some pictures. What about you?
I'm going to have an art lesson.
I'm going to learn how to swim.
I'm going to look for some beautiful leaves.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 我打算寻找一些漂亮的树叶。

knowledge_atoms.text: I'm going to see a film tomorrow.
I'm going to see a film.
I'm going to visit my grandparents.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 我打算去看望我的祖父母。

knowledge_atoms.text: John is going to buy his favourite comic book.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 约翰打算去买他最喜欢的漫画书。

knowledge_atoms.text: Just try. This way ... This way ... OK, now can you do it?
Mike is going to see a film.
Mike is going to see a film. John is going to buy his favourite comic book. Hmm, what am I going to do this weekend?
My aunt is going to make mooncakes.
My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 我的表弟杰克下周要来看我。

knowledge_atoms.text: My family are going to get together and have a big dinner.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 我的家人打算聚在一起吃顿大餐。

knowledge_atoms.text: Sounds great! I'm going to see a film tomorrow.
Stress on the first syllable (e.g., lesson, dinner)
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 重音在第一个音节（如：lesson, dinner）

knowledge_atoms.text: Stress on the second syllable (e.g., tonight, tomorrow)
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 重音在第二个音节（如：tonight, tomorrow）

knowledge_atoms.text: That's nice. Where are you going?
To the bookstore. I'm going to buy a new comic book.
We will all be together tonight.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 今晚我们将都聚在一起。

knowledge_atoms.text: We're going to draw some pictures in Renmin Park.
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 我们要去人民公园画画。

knowledge_atoms.text: We're going to see a film about space travel!
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 我们要去看一部关于太空旅行的电影！

knowledge_atoms.text: What am I going to do this weekend?
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 这个周末我打算做什么？

knowledge_atoms.text: What are you going to buy, Sarah?
What are you going to buy?
What are you going to do for Mid-Autumn Festival?
What are you going to do in the nature park?
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 你打算在自然公园里做什么？

knowledge_atoms.text: What are you going to do in your lesson?
What are you going to do there?
What are you going to do this morning?
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 你今天上午打算做什么？

knowledge_atoms.text: What are you going to do today?
What are you going to do tomorrow?
What are you going to do?
What do people do on these days?
knowledge_atoms.gloss: 人们在这些日子里做什么？

knowledge_atoms.text: Where are you going this afternoon?
Yes. Do you have comic books?

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. Chen Jie and Sarah are discussing their plans for the nature park. Below, Wu Binbin is looking at a schedule for 'Travel to Mars'.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word nature park?
Can you say: What are you going to do in the nature park?

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. Wu Binbin is lying on a sofa, thinking about his weekend plans. A thought bubble shows a street scene with a cinema and a bookstore.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word weekend plan?
Can you say: What am I going to do this weekend?

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. Mike and Sarah are talking on the phone about their plans for tomorrow.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word tomorrow?
Can you say: What are you going to do tomorrow?

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. Sarah and Chen Jie are talking about their plans. There are illustrations of four activities and a list of time expressions.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word visit my grandparents?
Can you say: What are you going to do today?

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. John and Amy are talking about John's cousin Jack and their plans to see a movie.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word visit?
Can you say: My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. Jack and John are walking past a cinema. They are talking about their destination.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word dictionary?
Can you say: Where are we going?

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. A diary entry written by Wu Binbin about his plans for the Mid-Autumn Festival, including an acrostic poem about FAMILY.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word Mid-Autumn Festival?
Can you say: My family are going to get together and have a big dinner.

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. A table to organize information about Wu Binbin's family's plans for the Mid-Autumn Festival based on the text from the previous page.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word Mid-Autumn Festival?
Can you say: be going to

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. A sentence building game using Russian nesting dolls (Matryoshka) to practice the 'be going to' structure.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word going to?
Can you say: What are you going to do?

page_lessons.page_intro_cn: Theme: My weekend plan. Zoom plans to learn how to swim but tries to do it by watching TV. Zip encourages him to go to the pool and learn by practice.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word disturb?
Can you say: What are you going to do tomorrow?

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Understand and say the core dialogue pattern on the page.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Chen Jie and Sarah are discussing their plans for the nature park. Below, Wu Binbin is looking at a schedule for 'Travel to Mars'. Key patterns: What are you going to do in the nature park?; I'm going to draw some pictures.
core_patterns: What are you going to do in the nature park?
I'm going to draw some pictures.
I'm going to look for some beautiful leaves.
I'm going to draw some pictures. What about you?
allowed_answer_scope: I'm going to look for some beautiful leaves.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word nature park?
Can you say: What are you going to do in the nature park?
return_anchors: What are you going to do in the nature park?
I'm going to draw some pictures.
I'm going to look for some beautiful leaves.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Understand and say the core dialogue pattern on the page.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Wu Binbin is lying on a sofa, thinking about his weekend plans. A thought bubble shows a street scene with a cinema and a bookstore. Key patterns: What am I going to do this weekend?; Mike is going to see a film.
core_patterns: What am I going to do this weekend?
Mike is going to see a film.
John is going to buy his favourite comic book.
Mike is going to see a film. John is going to buy his favourite comic book. Hmm, what am I going to do this weekend?
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word weekend plan?
Can you say: What am I going to do this weekend?
return_anchors: What am I going to do this weekend?
Mike is going to see a film.
John is going to buy his favourite comic book.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Catch the key information from the listening task.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: It's Saturday morning. Sarah is on the phone with Mike. Key patterns: Listen and circle.
core_patterns: Listen and circle.
allowed_answer_scope: A. He has homework to do.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word swimming?
Can you say: Listen and circle.
return_anchors: Listen and circle.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Understand and say the core dialogue pattern on the page.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Mike and Sarah are talking on the phone about their plans for tomorrow. Key patterns: What are you going to do tomorrow?; I'm going to have an art lesson.
core_patterns: What are you going to do tomorrow?
I'm going to have an art lesson.
We're going to draw some pictures in Renmin Park.
I'm going to see a film tomorrow.
What are you going to do in your lesson?
Sounds great! I'm going to see a film tomorrow.
allowed_answer_scope: Sarah is going to have an art lesson. Mike is going to see a film.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word tomorrow?
Can you say: What are you going to do tomorrow?
return_anchors: What are you going to do tomorrow?
I'm going to have an art lesson.
We're going to draw some pictures in Renmin Park.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Use the page dialogue pattern in short guided practice.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Practice talking about weekend plans using the given phrases. Key patterns: What is ... going to do?; What are ... going to do?
core_patterns: What is ... going to do?
What are ... going to do?
He/She is going to ...
They are going to ...
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word go ice-skating?
Can you say: What is ... going to do?
branchable_topics: Talk about your plan and your friends' plans for the weekend
return_anchors: What is ... going to do?
What are ... going to do?
He/She is going to ...

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Recognize and say the core vocabulary on the page.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Sarah and Chen Jie are talking about their plans. There are illustrations of four activities and a list of time expressions. Key patterns: What are you going to do today?; I'm going to see a film.
core_patterns: What are you going to do today?
I'm going to see a film.
allowed_answer_scope: I'm going to see a film.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word visit my grandparents?
Can you say: What are you going to do today?
return_anchors: What are you going to do today?
I'm going to see a film.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Transfer the page language into a new speaking task.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Students make a weekend plan by filling in a table and sharing it with a partner. Key patterns: What are you going to do this morning?; I'm going to visit my grandparents.
core_patterns: What are you going to do this morning?
I'm going to visit my grandparents.
allowed_answer_scope: What are you going to do this morning?
I'm going to visit my grandparents.
entry_probe_questions: Can you say: What are you going to do this morning?
return_anchors: What are you going to do this morning?
I'm going to visit my grandparents.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Catch the key information from the listening task.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: John is on his way home. He sees Amy. Listen and answer. Key patterns: Listen and answer.
core_patterns: Listen and answer.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word ice cream?
Can you say: Listen and answer.
return_anchors: Listen and answer.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Understand and say the core dialogue pattern on the page.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: John and Amy are talking about John's cousin Jack and their plans to see a movie. Key patterns: My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.; We're going to see a film about space travel!
core_patterns: My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.
We're going to see a film about space travel!
Why not go on Tuesday?
It's half price then!
That's nice. Where are you going?
Cool! I have lots of comic books about space. When are you going?
allowed_answer_scope: My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.
We're going to the cinema. We're going to see a film about space travel!
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word visit?
Can you say: My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.
return_anchors: My cousin Jack is going to visit me next week.
We're going to see a film about space travel!
Why not go on Tuesday?

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Work on the practice speaking activity.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: A follow-up question based on the dialogue. Key patterns: Answer the question.
core_patterns: Answer the question.
allowed_answer_scope: They are going to the cinema.
entry_probe_questions: Can you say: Answer the question.
return_anchors: Answer the question.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Transfer the page language into a new speaking task.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: A survey table to interview classmates about their future plans. Key patterns: Where are you going this afternoon?; What are you going to do there?
core_patterns: Where are you going this afternoon?
What are you going to do there?
When are you going?
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word this afternoon?
Can you say: Where are you going this afternoon?
branchable_topics: Interview your classmates and then give a report
return_anchors: Where are you going this afternoon?
What are you going to do there?
When are you going?

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Recognize and say the core vocabulary on the page.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Jack and John are walking past a cinema. They are talking about their destination. Key patterns: Where are we going?; To the bookstore.
core_patterns: Where are we going?
To the bookstore.
I'm going to buy a new comic book.
To the bookstore. I'm going to buy a new comic book.
allowed_answer_scope: To the bookstore. I'm going to buy a new comic book.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word dictionary?
Can you say: Where are we going?
return_anchors: Where are we going?
To the bookstore.
I'm going to buy a new comic book.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Use the page dialogue pattern in short guided practice.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Students are role-playing a shopping scene in a bookstore. Key patterns: Can I help you?; Do you have ...?
core_patterns: Can I help you?
Do you have ...?
What are you going to buy?
Yes. Do you have comic books?
What are you going to buy, Sarah?
Yes! Here they are!
allowed_answer_scope: I'm going to buy some word books.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word comic books?
Can you say: Can I help you?
return_anchors: Can I help you?
Do you have ...?
What are you going to buy?

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Work on the practice speaking activity.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Discussion about holidays based on three photos: National Day, Spring Festival, and Christmas. Key patterns: What are these holidays?; What do people do on these days?
core_patterns: What are these holidays?
What do people do on these days?
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word holiday?
Can you say: What are these holidays?
return_anchors: What are these holidays?
What do people do on these days?

teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: A diary entry written by Wu Binbin about his plans for the Mid-Autumn Festival, including an acrostic poem about FAMILY. Key patterns: My family are going to get together and have a big dinner.; My aunt is going to make mooncakes.

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Notice and repeat the target pronunciation pattern.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Pronunciation practice focusing on word stress patterns (first syllable vs second syllable). Key patterns: Stress on the first syllable (e.g., lesson, dinner); Stress on the second syllable (e.g., tonight, tomorrow)
core_patterns: Stress on the first syllable (e.g., lesson, dinner)
Stress on the second syllable (e.g., tonight, tomorrow)

Can you say: Stress on the first syllable (e.g., lesson, dinner)
return_anchors: Stress on the first syllable (e.g., lesson, dinner)

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Review and consolidate the core language point.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: A sentence building game using Russian nesting dolls (Matryoshka) to practice the 'be going to' structure. Key patterns: What are you going to do?; Where are you going?
core_patterns: What are you going to do?
Where are you going?
When are you going?
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word going to?
Can you say: What are you going to do?
return_anchors: What are you going to do?
Where are you going?
When are you going?

teaching_blocks.teaching_goal: Read and retell the key story events.
teaching_blocks.teaching_summary: Zoom plans to learn how to swim but tries to do it by watching TV. Zip encourages him to go to the pool and learn by practice. Key patterns: What are you going to do tomorrow?; I'm going to learn how to swim.
core_patterns: What are you going to do tomorrow?
I'm going to learn how to swim.
Learn by doing.
How can you learn to swim without going to a pool?
Just try. This way ... This way ... OK, now can you do it?
OK. I'll teach you.
allowed_answer_scope: I'm going to learn how to swim.
Please don't disturb me. I'm learning to swim.
Come on. Let's go to the swimming pool.
Just jump in! Catch this. Practise and you will learn.
entry_probe_questions: Do you know the word disturb?
Can you say: What are you going to do tomorrow?
return_anchors: What are you going to do tomorrow?
I'm going to learn how to swim.
Learn by doing.
